2605.17954.title: A More Word-like Image Tokenization for MLLMs
2605.17954.paper_summary: Modern multimodal large language models (MLLMs) typically keep the language model fixed and train a visual projector that maps the pixels into a sequence of tokens in its embedding space, so that images can be presented in essentially the same form as text. However, the language model has been optimized to operate on discrete, semantically meaningful tokens, while prevailing visual projectors transform an image into a long stream of continuous and highly correlated embeddings. This causes the visual tokens to behave differently from the word-like units that LLMs are originally trained to understand. We propose a novel Disentangled Visual Tokenization (DiVT) that clusters patch embeddings into coherent semantic units, so each token corresponds to a distinct visual concept instead of a rigid grid cell. DiVT further adapts its token budget to image complexity, providing an explicit accuracy-compute trade-off modifying neither the vision encoder nor the language model. Across diverse multimodal benchmarks, DiVT matches or surpasses baselines with significantly fewer visual tokens, demonstrating robustness under limited token budgets, significantly reducing memory cost and latency while making visual inputs more compatible with LLMs. Our code is available at https://github.com/snuviplab/DiVT.
2605.17954.paper_summary_zh: 現代的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）通常保持語言模型不變，並訓練一個視覺投影器，將像素映射到其嵌入空間中的一系列標記，以便圖像可以以與文本基本相同的形式呈現。<lb/>然而，語言模型已被優化以在離散的、具有語義意義的標記上運行，而現有的視覺投影器則將圖像轉換為一長串連續且高度相關的嵌入。<lb/>這導致視覺標記的行為與LLMs最初訓練理解的類似單詞單元有所不同。<lb/>我們提出了一種新穎的解耦視覺標記化（DiVT），將補丁嵌入聚類為一致的語義單元，使每個標記對應於一個獨特的視覺概念，而不是一個僵化的網格單元。<lb/>DiVT進一步根據圖像複雜性調整其標記預算，提供明確的準確性-計算權衡，既不修改視覺編碼器也不修改語言模型。<lb/>在各種多模態基準測試中，DiVT以顯著更少的視覺標記匹配或超越基準，顯示在有限的標記預算下的穩健性，顯著降低了內存成本和延遲，同時使視覺輸入與LLMs更兼容。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/snuviplab/DiVT 獲得。
2605.17954.author: Hyun Lee et.al.
2605.17954.authors: Hyun Lee, Hyemin Jeong, Yejin Kim, Hyungwook Choi, Hyunsoo Cho, Soo Kyung Kim, Joonseok Lee
